news.t: VanEck Announces Year-End Distributions for VanEck Equity ETFs
VanEck Announces Yearend Distributions for VanEck Equity ETFs

holdings.infoBox: HAP is an equity ETF with a total of 144 individual holdings. The top holdings are Deere & Company stock at 4.96%, Chevron at 4.72%, Exxon Mobil at 4.71%, Iberdrola at 4.09%, and NextEra Energy at 3.93%.

overview.description: The VanEck Natural Resources ETF (HAP) is an exchange-traded fund that mostly invests in stocks based on a particular theme. The fund tracks an index of companies from around the world with revenues derived from natural resources. The fund uses a tiered market-cap weighting scheme with fixed allocations for its sub-themes. HAP was launched on Aug 29, 2008 and is issued by VanEck.

data.title: ETF Edge: How investors are handling geopolitical uncertainty and energy pricing whiplash
data.text: Energy markets around the world have been shaken up by geopolitical volatility, most notably in Venezuela and Iran. However, there's much more to the story beyond just those two countries.

data.title: VanEck Announces Year-End Distributions for VanEck Equity ETFs
data.text: NEW YORK--(BUSINESS WIRE)--VanEck announced today the 2025 annual distributions per share for its VanEck equity exchange-traded funds.

data.title: VanEck Announces Yearend Distributions for VanEck Equity ETFs
data.text: NEW YORK--(BUSINESS WIRE)--VanEck announced today the following 2022 annual distributions per share for its VanEck® equity exchange-traded funds.
